failure_surface: Only 1 out of expected matches found; row count mismatch (processed 6 rows vs 5 data rows); potential sheet access issue.
root_cause: Agent accessed wb.active instead of explicitly selecting the known sheet name 'Munka1'. Also failed to tokenize multi-term search queries in comments (parameters are often part of compound strings like 'HU/table' separated by slashes or dashes, requiring substring/token matching rather than simple whole-word containment).
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before editing, verify the correct sheet by checking wb.sheetnames and selecting the target sheet programmatically. When searching for parameters in free-text comments, split comments on common delimiters (/,-, space, |) and normalize tokens for comparison rather than naive string containment.
evidence: Agent used 'ws = wb.active' without confirming which sheet contains the data, despite Sheet Name 'Munka1' being visible in spreadsheet_content
Code processed 'Total rows processed: 6' while spreadsheet_content shows only 5 data rows (indices 0-4)
Comments contain compound formats like 'HU/table', 'desk/UK', 'table/UK' - naive 'in' matching fails when parameters are embedded with delimiters
Only 1 match found ('uk, table') when multiple parameters should theoretically match based on data patterns
Parameters themselves may contain dashes per instructions ('search parameters themselves may contain dashes'), complicating substring matching

memory_items.title: Explicit Sheet Selection
memory_items.lesson: Never assume wb.active is the target sheet; always list available sheets programmatically and select the appropriate one by name before any read/write operations.
memory_items.generalization: When working with Excel workbooks containing multiple sheets or uncertain sheet naming, use wb.sheetnames to enumerate sheets and wb[desired_sheet_name] to access the correct worksheet programmatically.

memory_items.title: Tokenize Free-Text Search Queries
memory_items.lesson: When matching search parameters against unstructured comment text, split comments on common delimiters (spaces, dashes, slashes, pipes) and compare normalized tokens rather than using direct substring containment.
memory_items.generalization: For parameter extraction tasks from variable-format text fields, preprocess both search terms and target text by normalizing delimiters and comparing discrete tokens to handle inconsistent formatting.

memory_items.title: Validate Row Range Before Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Always verify the actual row count of data versus expectations before iteration to avoid off-by-one errors or including/excluding unintended rows.
memory_items.generalization: When manipulating specific cell ranges (e.g., G2:G5), calculate the expected row indices explicitly and log the actual ws.max_row value to confirm alignment with the answer_position specification.

edits.content:
## 6. Multi-Query Text Matching
- **Explicit Sheet Access**: Verify wb.sheetnames and select the target sheet by name (wb['SheetName']) instead of relying on wb.active.
- **Tokenize Delimited Content**: When matching search parameters in free-text fields, split on common separators (/, -, space, |, comma) and compare normalized tokens rather than plain substring matching.
- **Range Validation**: Log actual row counts before iteration and cross-reference with answer_position specifications to prevent off-by-one errors.
- **Edge Case Handling**: Account for parameters containing special characters that overlap with delimiters; use word boundary checks where applicable.
